description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed primarily from horizontally stacked logs with visible rounded log ends at the corners. The roof is a steeply pitched gable style, covered in wooden planks, with a pronounced overhang and a decorative red trim beneath the eaves. The front facade features a small covered porch area, a single window, and a central door, while the side view reveals another small window. The rear and one side of the cabin are windowless, emphasizing the solid, insulated nature of the structure. The overall appearance is traditional and robust, evoking a classic woodland or rural retreat. The materials and design suggest durability and a cozy, functional interior.
description: A traditional log cabin with a steep gable roof, visible log construction, and a small covered porch at the front. The exterior features wood textures, red trim, and minimal windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gable roof, covered porch, central door, and a small window to the right.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long wooden roof, a single small window, and the log construction at the corners.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the solid log wall, red decorative trim under the roof, and no windows or doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain log wall, no windows or doors, and the extended roof overhang.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with steep pitch, wooden plank covering, pronounced overhang, and decorative red trim under the eaves
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical arrangement with one small rectangular window on the front facade and one on the right side; no windows on the rear or left side
attribute_door_placement: single exterior door centered on the front facade beneath the porch roof
